name: Playwright
what_it_does: End-to-end browser testing and automation framework by Microsoft supporting Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit with auto-waiting, network interception, trace viewer, and an official MCP server for AI-driven browser control.
use_cases: Write and run end-to-end tests across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit from a single test suite using Playwright's cross-browser API
Use the official Playwright MCP server to give AI agents authenticated browser control for web scraping, form filling, and UI verification tasks
Capture screenshots, record video, and generate traces of test runs for visual regression testing and debugging
Intercept and mock network requests during tests to isolate frontend behavior from backend services
Test authentication flows, multi-tab interactions, and file uploads using Playwright's built-in context and page management
not_for: Unit or component-level testing where a full browser is unnecessary overhead — use Vitest or Jest instead
Chromium-only scraping pipelines where Puppeteer's lighter footprint and direct Chrome DevTools Protocol access is preferred
Mobile native app testing which requires a different toolchain such as Appium or Detox
best_when: Running cross-browser end-to-end tests or giving AI agents a reliable, auto-waiting browser control interface with network inspection capabilities.
avoid_when: The use case requires only Chromium and the lighter Puppeteer API surface is sufficient, or the team needs unit-level test isolation without browser overhead.

auth.notes: Library with no network auth layer; browser authentication state can be saved and reused across test runs via storageState

pricing.notes: Completely free and open source; Microsoft Playwright Testing is a separate paid cloud service for parallel test execution

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Playwright produces detailed error messages with DOM snapshots, action traces, and suggested fixes for common timeout and locator failures; trace viewer provides step-by-step visual replay
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only page inspections are idempotent; actions that mutate application state (form submissions, clicks) are not idempotent and require test isolation via browser contexts
known_agent_gotchas: Browser binaries (~300MB per browser) must be installed via npx playwright install; agents provisioning environments must run this step before test execution
Auto-waiting is tied to actionability checks; agents must not add manual waits that conflict with Playwright's built-in waiting strategy
page.evaluate() runs in the browser context and cannot access Node.js variables directly; data must be serialized and passed as arguments
Multiple browser contexts share no state by default; agents that test multi-user flows must explicitly create and manage separate contexts
The MCP server exposes full browser control including navigation to arbitrary URLs; sandbox or allowlist network access when using in automated agent pipelines

security_score_components.security_notes: MCP server grants broad browser control and should be sandboxed with network allowlists in agent pipelines; storageState files contain session cookies and must be protected like credentials; headless mode does not prevent SSRF via browser navigation
